items.title: AME Reit to acquire RM50.2mil Johor industrial land in maiden third-party deal
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR: AME Real Estate Investment Trust (AME Reit) is acquiring three parcels of vacant freehold industrial land measuring a combined 3.59 hectares at i-Park @ Senai Airport City, Johor, for RM50.2 million, marking its first third-party acquisition since its listing. In a statement today, AME Reit said RHB Trustees Bhd, acting on its behalf, had entered into a sale and purchase agreement (SPA) for the proposed acquisition. The transaction marks AME Reit's first third-party acquisition since its listing, following RM289.5 million in completed asset acquisitions from its sponsor, AME Elite Consortium Bhd.

items.title: Abang Johari: AirBorneo’s first international flight marks new era for Sarawak as regional economic hub
items.summary: SIBU, July 23 — AirBorneo’s inaugural international flight from Kuching to Singapore on Wednesday marked a historic milestone in Sarawak’s aviation development, said Datuk Patinggi Tan Sri Abang Johari Tun Openg. The Premier said this during the Sarawak Day 2026 Celebration at Dataran Tun Tuanku Haji Bujang here on Wednesday night. Abang Johari also envisioned AirBorneo helping to promote Sarawak’s unique local products to international travellers, including dabai (Sarawak olive) from Song and Kanowit.

items.title: Four Kelantan mosques hit by donation box thefts
items.summary: TUMPAT: Police are investigating the theft of donation boxes from four mosques in the district over a two-day period between July 20 and yesterday. Tumpat police chief Assistant Commissioner Mohd Khairi Shafie said the cases occurred at Masjid Al Aziz Al Hadi in Mukim Kota Warisan and Masjid Nurul Hidayah in Kampung Gerong, Palekbang. Mohd Khairi said police were actively tracking down those responsible for the thefts.

items.title: Penang's 1926 Heritage Hotel reopens
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR: A century-old heritage property in Penang has reopened as a boutique urban resort, underscoring the growing appeal of heritage-led hospitality as Malaysia's domestic tourism continues to expand. The redevelopment transformed the property into a 78-room hotel spanning 24 interconnected heritage link houses. The launch also coincides with robust growth in Malaysia's domestic tourism sector.

items.title: Resilient economy, policy support drive outlook, stronger Q2 growth
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR: Malaysia's resilient economic outlook, underpinned by targeted government measures, has helped lift second-quarter (Q2) growth above expectations despite heightened global uncertainties, Treasury secretary-general Tan Sri Johan Mahmood Merican said. Johan said the stronger performance reflected the effectiveness of the government's economic management and targeted initiatives in cushioning the economy against external headwinds. "This shows that the government's economic management and the measures implemented, such as the targeted diesel subsidy and initiatives to increase financing for SMEs, clearly demonstrate that these measures have helped support the momentum of economic growth and the country's economic stability.

items.title: Desa Lestari revamp aims for RM2,300 household income
items.summary: GEORGE TOWN: The government will revamp the Desa Lestari programme, targeting a monthly household income of RM2,300 for participants through a strengthened implementation framework. Deputy Prime Minister and Rural and Regional Development Minister Datuk Seri Dr Ahmad Zahid Hamidi said the decade-old programme would undergo more than a rebranding exercise, with reforms aimed at improving governance, expanding market access and delivering sustainable economic gains for rural communities. The Rural Development Ministers' Council (MEXCLUB) has agreed to strengthen Desa Lestari as a key catalyst for rural economic growth and household income, with a special task force to undertake structured interventions involving participating cooperatives.

items.title: Umno leaders cool to Hamzah Zainudin’s ‘Perikatan Barisan Nasional’ proposal
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR, July 23 — Several Umno leaders have expressed reservations over Opposition Leader Datuk Seri Hamzah Zainudin’s proposal to name the Barisan Nasional (BN)-Perikatan Nasional (PN) cooperation “Perikatan Barisan Nasional”. Instead, he said the BN-PN partnership was merely an understanding to contest the Negeri Sembilan state election together. “Let us work together with mutual understanding, avoid overlapping seats and win the Negeri Sembilan state election together.

items.title: AirBorneo launches first international route with Kuching-Singapore flight
items.summary: KUCHING, July 23 — AirBorneo Airways (AirBorneo) launched its first international route on Sarawak Day, connecting Kuching and Singapore through Singapore Changi Airport Terminal 4. Marking the airline’s entry into international operations, Flight MY363 departed Kuching at 6.50pm and arrived in Singapore at 8.04pm, 21 minutes ahead of schedule, said AirBorneo in a statement yesterday. AirBorneo chairman Datuk Amar Mohamad Abu Bakar Marzuki said the inaugural international route marked a significant milestone for both the airline and Sarawak.

items.title: Aminuddin: 'I will work '200pct to ensure PH's victory'
items.summary: PORT DICKSON: Negri Sembilan Pakatan Harapan chairman Datuk Seri Aminuddin Harun said he will work "200 per cent" to ensure the coalition's victory. This, after PH chairman and Prime Minister Datuk Seri Anwar Ibrahim confirmed him as the menteri besar candidate if the coalition successfully retains control of the state government. Aminuddin, who is PH's Linggi candidate, said the announcement was not new, as it had already been made several times since the launch of the election machinery.

items.title: A good problem to have? TVET boom leaves polytechnic hostels bursting at the seams, Zambry says fixes on the way
items.summary: SEPANG, July 23 — The government will address the issue of insufficient accommodation facilities at several polytechnics by implementing improvement measures, including repairing hostels identified as requiring attention, said Higher Education Minister Datuk Seri Dr Zambry Abd Kadir. He said the accommodation issue emerged following a significant increase in applications to polytechnics and skills institutions as a result of the government’s efforts to encourage more Sijil Pelajaran Malaysia (SPM) holders to pursue technical and vocational education and training (TVET). Zambry said the increase in student admissions had resulted in accommodation constraints at several polytechnics, particularly institutions with older hostel buildings requiring maintenance.

items.title: Hospital sexual harassment claims will not be ignored, says Sabah minister
items.summary: KOTA KINABALU: The Sabah Women, Health and People's Wellbeing Ministry is looking into allegations of sexual harassment at a hospital here and is coordinating action with the federal authorities. ""This matter is now under the purview of the Health Ministry and the Sabah Health Department. Sabah Police Commissioner Datuk Jauteh Dikun said police were investigating an alleged sexual harassment incident involving a hospital director that occurred in October last year.

items.title: Tanjong Sepat to be free of pig farms by October, says Selangor exco
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR, July 23 — The clearance of all pig farms in Tanjong Sepat, Kuala Langat is expected to be completed by October, bringing pig farming operations in the area to an end. Selangor Infrastructure and Agriculture Committee chairman Datuk Izham Hashim said the timeline would allow the remaining piglets in the area to reach maturity at seven to eight months of age before being disposed of, Kosmo! He said about 12,000 pigs remain in Tanjong Sepat, down from around 240,000 before the phased clearance exercise began.

items.title: Sabah dismemberment murder accused fit to stand trial
items.summary: KOTA KINABALU: A septuagenarian charged with murdering a woman whose dismembered body parts were found in Indah Permai, Sepanggar, has been found fit to stand trial, the magistrate's court heard today. Deputy Public Prosecutor Akaash Singh informed magistrate Dzul Elmy Yunus of the findings when the case came up for mention to update the court on the status of the psychiatric evaluation report on the accused, Piluta Samad, 72. On May 15, the court granted a one-month extension for the psychiatric evaluation.

items.title: PH, BN in neck-and-neck race for Negri Sembilan, says Vodus Research
items.summary: According to Vodus Research's latest forecast, PH leads the statewide popular vote with 42 per cent, ahead of BN at 35 per cent and Perikatan Nasional (PN) at seven per cent. Independent candidates account for two per cent of the popular vote, while 14 per cent of respondents remain undecided. Among Bumiputera voters, BN leads with 41 per cent, followed by PH at 27 per cent and PN at 25 per cent.

items.title: Terengganu’s tourism boom: 6.5 million visitors bring up to RM1.5b in revenue
items.summary: KUALA TERENGGANU, July 23 — Terengganu recorded the arrival of 6.5 million tourists, generating revenue between RM1.4 and RM1.5 billion, in the first six months of this year. State Tourism, Culture, Environment, and Climate Change Committee chairman Datuk Razali Idris said that it involves 70 per cent domestic tourists and 30 per cent international tourists holidaying on resort islands and other tourist destinations. Based on the Domestic Tourism Survey 2025, released by the Department of Statistics Malaysia (DOSM), Terengganu received 15.46 million domestic visitors in 2025, an increase from 14.46 million visitors the previous year, representing a growth of 6.9 per cent.

items.title: Betamek's Q1 earnings jump 53pct on stronger sales
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR: Betamek Bhd's net profit rose 53.2 per cent to RM8.25 million in the first quarter ended June 30, 2026 from RM5.39 million a year ago. The performance was lifted by higher production volumes across Betamek's automotive product segments, expanding export sales and continued progress in customer diversification. Connectivity modules continued to gain traction and contributed RM6.77 million, reflecting Betamek's growing participation in higher electronics-content vehicle platforms.

items.title: Marriott to open Courtyard Penang Icon City in 2026 after the Iconic Hotel revamp
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR: Marriott International is expanding in Penang with the opening of Courtyard by Marriott Penang Icon City in December 2026, following the transformation of the existing Iconic Hotel Penang. Marriott International vice president of hotel development for Asia Pacific excluding China, Andree Susilo, said Malaysia remains a key growth market for the group, driven by strong economic fundamentals and evolving travel demand. Iconic Group chairman Datuk Seri Tan Kean Tet said the signing marked another milestone in the company's partnership with Marriott International.

items.title: Chief Secretary steps in after complaints pile up at Universiti Malaya Medical Centre
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR, July 23 — Chief Secretary to the Government (KSN) Tan Sri Datuk Seri Shamsul Azri Abu Bakar has directed that immediate measures be taken to address several critical service issues affecting the Universiti Malaya Medical Centre (PPUM). In a Facebook post today, Shamsul Azri said safeguarding public health remains a top priority of the MADANI Government and had called for a meeting this morning to discuss the matter. According to Shamsul Azri, the meeting focused on addressing policy-related issues arising from public complaints, including long waiting times, challenges in the delivery of clinical services, and issues related to skilled manpower and specialist expertise.

items.title: Zahid: Formalise PN-BN proposal if serious
items.summary: GEORGE TOWN: Datuk Seri Dr Ahmad Zahid Hamidi today said any proposal for cooperation between Barisan Nasional (BN) and Perikatan Nasional (PN) should be formally discussed. The BN chairman said the proposal had only been raised in a campaign speech and had not been discussed through official channels. Zahid was responding to a question on a reported proposal involving cooperation between PN and BN.

items.title: Climate change takes toll as 350km of Selangor coastline erodes, state exco says
items.summary: SHAH ALAM, July 23 — About 350km of Selangor’s coastline has been identified as affected by erosion due to various factors, including climate change. Selangor Infrastructure and Agriculture Committee chairman Datuk Izham Hashim said coastal defence structures, including wave barriers, have been installed along about 250km of the affected areas, according to a report published by Kosmo! Izham said this after officiating the review workshop for the Selangor Integrated Coastal Management Plan here today.

items.title: Businesses brace for tougher second half
items.summary: Its Malaysia Business and Economic Conditions Survey (M-BECS) found that business sentiment weakened in the first half of 2026 and is expected to remain subdued in the second half of the year. Lee said SMEs represented 91.4 per cent of surveyed firms, comprising 23.5 per cent micro enterprises and 50.4 per cent small businesses, underscoring the important role of MSMEs in Malaysia's economy. About 34.4 per cent of respondents said economic conditions had worsened, while 34.6 per cent reported a deterioration in the business environment.

items.title: Sabah aims to boost local meat supply threefold by 2030
items.summary: KOTA KINABALU, July 23 — The Sabah government aims to increase the state’s ruminant meat Self-Sufficiency Ratio (SSR) to 25 per cent by 2030, up from the current 7.05 per cent, the Sabah State Legislative Assembly was told today. Sabah Assistant Minister of Agriculture, Fisheries and Food Industry Datuk Hendrus Anding said the state government has undertaken various initiatives to achieve the target, including allocating RM24.3 million to upgrade government-owned livestock farms across the state. Elaborating, Hendrus said the upgrading programme is expected to increase large ruminant meat production by 199 per cent, from 126 head this year to 377 head annually by 2030, while breeding stock output is projected to rise by 78 per cent to 752 head a year.

items.title: Zahid dismisses talk of bid to topple Anwar
items.summary: GEORGE TOWN: Datuk Seri Dr Ahmad Zahid Hamidi today dismissed speculation of any attempt to unseat Prime Minister Datuk Seri Anwar Ibrahim, reaffirming Barisan Nasional's commitment to the unity government until the end of the 15th Parliament. Responding to questions over claims of a plot to bring down the government, the BN chairman said there had been various forms of speculation but stressed that he had no intention of supporting any such move. "When we committed ourselves to the unity government, following the Yang di-Pertuan Agong's decree, we committed to remain until the end of the 15th Parliament.

items.title: Police in Wisconsin shoot man dead, online video of killing sparks protests
items.summary: Police said the officer who fired his weapon was injured by a knife that the man produced during the struggle. "Madison Police shot another black man. We need all eyes on Madison police.

items.title: Malaysia nearing renewable energy target as solar push gathers pace, says DPM Fadillah
items.summary: PASIR MAS, July 23 — Malaysia’s renewable energy installed capacity stood at nearly 32 per cent as of June, putting the country firmly on course to achieve its 35 per cent target by 2030, said Deputy Prime Minister Datuk Seri Fadillah Yusof. He said this achievement reflects the nation’s steady progress in driving its energy transition agenda under the National Energy Transition Roadmap (NETR). “Energy transition is not an option.

items.title: Meta employees' lawsuit shows that if AI fires you, proving it is the hard part
items.summary: A novel lawsuit claiming that Meta Platforms relied on discriminatory AI tools to select employees for layoffs highlights the problems workers face in suing employers over the new technology, including proving how it was actually used. Legal experts say workers often have little understanding of how AI systems are used in the workplace and many have also signed away their right to sue in court, agreeing instead to resolve workplace disputes through a private process called arbitration that can keep such claims from ever being tested publicly. Webber's firm is not involved in the Meta case.

items.title: Zahid replies to PM with pantun on 'roof and storm'
items.summary: GEORGE TOWN: Deputy Prime Minister Datuk Seri Dr Ahmad Zahid Hamidi today responded to Prime Minister Datuk Seri Anwar Ibrahim's unity-themed pantun with a four-line Malay verse of his own. He told reporters it was composed shortly after Anwar's post. Reading the pantun during a press conference here, Zahid said he wrote it at 9.04pm after seeing news reports of the prime minister's posting.

items.title: Seremban’s Terminal 1 earns Grade A rating, meets highest public transport standards, says Anthony Loke
items.summary: SEREMBAN, July 23 — Seremban’s Terminal 1 has received a Grade A rating from the Land Public Transport Agency (APAD), signifying that it meets the highest standards in management, operations, safety and user facilities. Transport Minister Anthony Loke said the recognition was not awarded to every terminal, as facilities must fulfil various criteria including cleanliness levels, operational efficiency, ticketing systems and passenger comfort, Astro Awani reported today. Loke said this after officiating the handover ceremony for the Grade A Terminal Rating to Seremban Terminal 1 today.

items.title: 3 women charged over viral 'Superman' stunt
items.summary: KUANTAN: Three women, including two underage girls, were charged in the magistrate's court today with dangerous riding after performing 'Superman' stunts on their motorcycles along Jalan Kuantan Bypass on July 16. Nur Liana Atirah Roslan, 21, claimed trial after the charge was read before magistrate Wahidah Zainal Abidin. They allegedly committed the offences on their respective Yamaha LC135 motorcycles along Jalan Kuantan Bypass, between Terminal Sentral Kuantan and Bukit Rangin, at about 12.30am on July 16.

items.title: Pan Am plane lost for 74 years found on Atlantic seabed
items.summary: Some 74 years after a Pan American Airways flight crashed into the water off the coast of Puerto Rico, an aviation search team said it found the wreckage sitting on the ocean floor. The Air/Sea Heritage Foundation and others, including Discovery Channel's series "Expedition Unknown," found the wreckage of the Pan Am "Clipper Endeavor" at the bottom of the Atlantic Ocean off Puerto Rico's northern coast last month, Discovery said on Tuesday. The Douglas DC-4 aircraft broke into two sections and came to rest after crashing into the ocean shortly after taking off from Puerto Rico on April 11, 1952, with 64 passengers and five crew members on board.

items.title: Perak MB urges youths to think critically, uphold ethics as AI transforms society
items.summary: IPOH, July 23 — Perak Menteri Besar Datuk Seri Saarani Mohamad today urged young generation to think critically, evaluate information responsibly and make ethical decisions as artificial intelligence (AI), automation and the digital economy reshape the future of work and society. Saarani said education must go beyond academic achievement by equipping the younger generation with critical thinking, problem-solving skills and a strong sense of responsibility. He said youths must be provided with the knowledge, training, guidance and opportunities to innovate as they will become future technology developers, policymakers, entrepreneurs and leaders shaping the world.

items.title: Westports Q2 profit jumps 56pct, declares 15 sen dividend
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR: Westports Holdings Bhd's net profit jumped 56 per cent to RM360.9 million in the second quarter of financial year 2026 (Q2 FY26) from RM231.6 million a year earlier. Revenue also grew 25 per cent to RM866.9 million from RM691 million, driven by higher tariffs, according to a bourse filing. Westports' net profit for the six-month period rose 51 per cent to RM687.4 million from RM454 million previously, while revenue surged 34 per cent to RM1.76 billion from RM1.3 billion.

items.title: ACCCIM survey finds Malaysian businesses still struggling with financing access, alternative channels underutilised
items.summary: Socio-Economic Research Centre (SERC) executive director Lee Heng Guie said 57.8 per cent of respondents in the M-BECS indicated that they sought financing primarily to support working capital and cash flow needs. As alternative financing channels remain underutilised, Lee said more than half of the respondents, or 54.5 per cent, felt that access to financing had become tighter compared with two years ago. From the findings, Lee said high interest or financing costs and lengthy approval processes remained the most significant barriers to obtaining financing, with 44.5 per cent of respondents citing these challenges.

items.title: No new refugee registrations for now as Malaysia reviews management process, Senate told
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR, July 23 — The government has directed the United Nations High Commissioner for Refugees (UNHCR) to halt all refugee registrations temporarily to allow for the restructuring of the management system via the Refugee Registration Document (DPP) programme. Deputy Foreign Minister Datuk Lukanisman Awang Sauni said the decision was made by the Cabinet to ensure that the management and registration of refugees in the country are carried out in a more orderly manner under government supervision. He said Malaysia is also continuing a multilateral approach towards Myanmar and Asean member countries to address the Rohingya refugee issue more seriously.

items.title: Sabah NGO president claims trial to MACC charge over RM2m Rungus cultural hall project
items.summary: KOTA KINABALU, July 23 — Pertubuhan Rungus Bersatu Sabah (PRBS) president Makinsos Nawai pleaded not guilty in the Kota Kinabalu Special Corruption Court today to a charge of abusing his position in connection with a RM2 million state-funded cultural hall project. The 67-year-old is accused of using his position as PRBS president in 2022 to obtain a RM2 million allocation from the Sabah Ministry of Finance for the construction of the Rungus Cultural Hall and Gallery in Kota Marudu. The alleged offence took place in 2022 at the Rungus Cultural Hall and Gallery in Kota Marudu.

items.title: Six candidates in race to lead UN set for televised debate
items.summary: NEW YORK: The six candidates for United Nations secretary-general will debate Thursday in the symbolic General Assembly Hall, one week before the formal start of a selection process crucial to an organisation in deep crisis. "This UN town hall will ... provide a rare opportunity to engage with the ideas, priorities and leadership vision of the candidates for one of the world's most important positions," General Assembly President Annalena Baerbock said. The candidates have already faced three hours of questioning each from representatives of the 193 UN member states.

items.title: Takiyuddin: Viral memo urging vote for BN is fake
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR: Pas secretary-general Datuk Seri Takiyuddin Hassan has denied instructing party members to vote for Barisan Nasional (BN) in the Negri Sembilan elections. Takiyuddin said a media statement circulating online, purportedly bearing such instructions along with the Pas letterhead and his name, is fake. The fabricated statement also instructed party members to ensure their family members vote for BN to secure Pakatan Harapan's defeat in the state polls.

items.title: Deadly wildfires force thousands from homes in parts of Europe
items.summary: ROME: Wildfires swept across parts of Italy, France and Spain on Wednesday, killing three firefighters and forcing thousands to flee as scorching temperatures and strong winds fuelled the blazes. In Sicily, around 6,000 firefighters, forest guards and civil protection officials battled dozens of fires that have raged for days across the Mediterranean island. Regional president Renato Schifani said about 50 fires were still burning despite the deployment of water-bombing aircraft.

items.title: Depression, schizophrenia among conditions covered under Socso’s Invalidity Scheme, says HR minister
items.summary: Human Resources Minister Datuk Seri R. Ramanan said these conditions are categorised as health conditions that do not fall under occupational diseases or those caused by workplace factors, Kosmo! He said illnesses arising from exposure to specific workplace risk factors are instead covered under the Employment Injury Scheme under the Employees’ Social Security Act 1969 (Act 4) and classified as occupational diseases. Ramanan was responding to Senator Datuk Seri S. Vell Paari Samy Vellu, who asked whether Perkeso planned to review its guidelines on recognising occupational diseases to include chronic illnesses caused by workplace stress and long-term occupational factors.

items.title: Stranger buys treats for children while celebrating daughter's pregnancy
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR: A man who bought snacks and ice cream for children at a convenience store was celebrating his daughter's pregnancy after years of waiting, according to a social media post. In the post, she said her children were choosing their food when the man, whom she referred to as "uncle", approached them and gave each child a KitKat. She said the man later returned to the store and surprised the children by buying each of them an ice cream and a box of Milo drinks.

items.title: Malaysia directs UNHCR to stop refugee registration for now
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR: The government has directed the United Nations High Commissioner for Refugees (UNHCR) to halt all refugee registrations temporarily to allow for the restructuring of the management system via the Refugee Registration Document (DPP) programme. Deputy Foreign Minister Datuk Lukanisman Awang Sauni said the decision was made by the Cabinet to ensure that the management and registration of refugees in the country are carried out in a more orderly manner under government supervision. He said Malaysia is also continuing a multilateral approach towards Myanmar and Asean member countries to address the Rohingya refugee issue more seriously.

items.title: Weather alert: MetMalaysia warns of thunderstorms, heavy rain for KL, Putrajaya, eight states until 3pm
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR, July 23 — The Malaysian Meteorological Department (MetMalaysia) has issued a warning of thunderstorms, heavy rain and strong winds in Kuala Lumpur, Putrajaya, and eight other states until 3pm today. MetMalaysia, in a statement, announced that the states affected are Kedah involving Yan, Pendang, Kuala Muda, Sik, Baling, Kulim and Bandar Baharu; as well as Perak involving Kerian and Larut, Matang and Selama. The same weather conditions are expected to hit the entire Penang, Selangor (Kuala Selangor, Klang, Gombak, Petaling, Kuala Langat, Hulu Langat and Sepang) and Pahang (Rompin), Negeri Sembilan (Seremban, Port Dickson, Rembau and Tampin), Melaka and Johor (Tangkak, Segamat, Muar, Batu Pahat and Kluang).

items.title: ACCCIM calls for tougher forced labour rules
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR: The Associated Chinese Chambers of Commerce and Industry of Malaysia (ACCCIM) has urged the government to strengthen laws and enforcement to stop imports linked to forced labour. This is to address a regulatory gap identified by the United States Trade Representative (USTR) related to forced labour. Malaysia falls under the lower 10 per cent tariff category due to commitments undertaken under its Agreement on Reciprocal Trade relating to forced labour import prohibitions.

items.title: Anwar calls for economic growth and tech progress that serve people, not just wealth
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR, July 23 — Malaysia must remain competitive, productive and open to investments while ensuring its economic growth is driven by a moral purpose, Prime Minister Datuk Seri Anwar Ibrahim said today. Anwar said technological advancements must pave the way for inclusive development that is rooted in tradition, culture and values and offers people a life with dignity. “Let Malaysia demonstrate that economic pragmatism and moral purpose can walk together.

items.title: Thailand assures Malaysians of safety amid southern border attacks
items.summary: KOTA BARU: Thailand has assured Malaysians that security remains a top priority in its southern border provinces despite ongoing insurgent attacks, including incidents in areas bordering Malaysia. Thailand's Consul-General in Kelantan, Phasit Chudabuddhi, said Thai security authorities had stepped up monitoring and would continue taking all necessary measures to ensure the safety of Malaysians travelling to the kingdom. He said Thai authorities remained committed to ensuring the safety and security of all visitors, including Malaysians travelling to southern Thailand.

items.title: BNP Paribas announces rocketing Q2 profit
items.summary: FRANCE: French banking giant BNP Paribas on Thursday announced almost-record profits for the second quarter this year, driven by the sale of its stake in a Belgian insurance firm. The bank said it had made 4.3 billion euros (US$4.9 billion) this quarter, hailing the performance of its asset management division, which was boosted by last year's acquisition of AXA IM. The banking giant's net profit surged 33.4 per cent year-on-year, in line with expectations from analysts surveyed by financial news agency Bloomberg and data provider FactSet.

items.title: Sabah strengthens efforts to manage human-crocodile conflict
items.summary: KOTA KINABALU: Sabah is adopting an integrated approach to address human-crocodile conflict by bringing together resources, expertise and personnel. Sabah's extensive waterways and coastal areas have made human-crocodile conflict a recurring challenge, leading to stronger coordination among agencies. Dr Andi said since 2023, the state government had strengthened mitigation efforts through the establishment of the Sabah State Crocodile Mitigation Operations Committee, chaired by the Sabah State Affairs Office secretary.

items.title: Police investigating sexual harassment allegations involving Sabah hospital director
items.summary: KOTA KINABALU, July 23 — Police have opened investigation papers into sexual harassment allegations by a public hospital director here after receiving a report in January, said Sabah police commissioner Datuk Jauteh Dikun. “The case only gained attention on social media yesterday, but police investigations had already been initiated under Section 509 of the Penal Code,” he said after the Sabah Police Contingent Headquarters monthly gathering today. In the video, they said there were various allegations by more than one person including one “rather serious” one.

items.title: Customs collects RM43.62b revenue in first half of 2026
items.summary: PUTRAJAYA: The Customs Department collected RM43.62 billion in revenue from January to June this year, exceeding the Finance Ministry's half-year target of RM40 billion. Its director-general Datuk Amran Ahmad said the collection surpassed the target by RM3.62 billion, or 109.05 per cent, and was 22.22 per cent higher than the RM35.69 billion recorded during the same period last year. Amran said cigarettes accounted for the highest number of cases, with 1,494 cases involving 1.24 billion sticks and total seizures, including duties and taxes, valued at RM329.27 million.

items.title: Tabung Haji stronger now after reforms, RCI report to be released soon, says Anwar
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR, July 23 — Prime Minister Datuk Seri Anwar Ibrahim today said the government previously deferred from publishing the Royal Commission of Inquiry’s (RCI) findings on The Pilgrims’ Fund Board (Tabung Haji-TH) to avoid creating panic among depositors. However, he said the government is now ready to make the RCI report public after restructuring efforts has placed the institution on a stronger financial footing. Anwar said he will consult the chief secretary to the government on whether the report requires Cabinet’s approval before it can be made public.

items.title: JPN denies viral claims of MyKad address enforcement campaign
items.summary: PUTRAJAYA, July 23 — The National Registration Department (JPN) is not conducting any special campaign or operations to require the public to update their residential address on their MyKad, nor to take action against those who have yet to do so. “If there is a change to a new and permanent residential address, cardholders are encouraged to visit JPN to update that information,” the department clarified in a social media post today, adding that the fee for replacing a MyKad is RM10. Under Regulation 15 of the National Registration Regulations 1990, if a person changes their residence and stays at the new address for 90 days or more, they must update their identification card to reflect the new address.

items.title: Beyond campaign promises, Negeri Sembilan voters seek leaders who can get things done
items.summary: SEREMBAN, July 23 — Entering the sixth day of the campaign period for the 16th Negeri Sembilan state election, a clear sentiment is emerging among the local electorate: they want leaders who are not just approachable, but who can actually get things done on the ground. A Bernama survey found that voters are looking for candidates who can do more than just carry their voices to the State Legislative Assembly; they want practical, workable solutions to the issues that hit closest to home. Meanwhile, 37-year-old trader Nurulainee Hamidon hopes that whichever party or candidate earns the mandate to form the next government will pay serious attention to the cost of living, an issue she describes as an ongoing and pressing struggle for ordinary people.

items.title: RTM named official broadcaster for 2026 Glasgow Commonwealth Games
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR, July 23 — After the success of its live coverage of the 2026 Fifa World Cup matches, Radio Televisyen Malaysia (RTM) will now be the official broadcaster of the 2026 Glasgow Commonwealth Games, which will be held from tomorrow (July 23) until August 2. RTM is scheduled to provide comprehensive coverage of the prestigious event, starting with the opening ceremony (at 3am Friday Malaysian time) until the closing ceremony of the quadrennial international multi-sport Games. The two daily live reporting sessions comprise a morning session from July 24 to August 2 from 6pm to 9pm (Malaysian time), followed by the evening session from July 25 to August 2 from 1am to 4am (Malaysian time), as Malaysia is seven hours ahead of Glasgow.

items.title: Penang plans next heritage makeover with east seafront upgrade after Fort Cornwallis, Beach Street works
items.summary: GEORGE TOWN, July 23 — Penang will continue with its infrastructure upgrading works along the eastern seafront after completing restoration and upgrading works along the north seafront. Penang Chief Minister Chow Kon Yeow said the restoration of Fort Cornwallis, along the north seafront, is set to be officially opened this Saturday. Penang Chief Minister Chow Kon Yeow delivering his speech at the grand launch of 1926 Heritage Hotel Penang.

items.title: Unsubsidised petrol prices rise 20 sen, diesel up 35 sen from today
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR, July 23 — Retail prices of unsubsidised RON97 and RON95 will increase by 20 sen per litre each, while unsubsidised diesel will rise by 35 sen per litre for the period from July 23 to 29 following higher global crude oil prices driven by escalating geopolitical tensions in West Asia. The retail price of unsubsidised diesel has also been revised to RM4.42 per litre from RM4.07 previously. According to MOF, unsubsidised petroleum product prices were influenced by movements in the global oil market after Brent crude prices rose 16 per cent last week following renewed tensions between the United States and Iran after a ceasefire expired.

items.title: Jalaluddin Alias among candidates for N. Sembilan MB if BN-Perikatan wins, says Hamzah Zainudin
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR, July 23 — Negeri Sembilan Umno chairman Datuk Seri Jalaluddin Alias is among the candidates being considered for the post of menteri besar if the Barisan Nasional (BN)-Perikatan Nasional (PN) alliance wins the state election on August 1, said PN deputy chairman Datuk Seri Hamzah Zainudin. However, Hamzah said BN and PN must first discuss and jointly decide on the candidate before making an announcement to voters, Harian Metro reported. He acknowledged that choosing the Negeri Sembilan menteri besar candidate was currently “slightly” challenging but assured that PN would support whoever BN puts forward for the post.

items.title: Foreign national arrested at Miri airport over suspected Brunei ATM skimming scheme
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR, July 23 — A foreign national wanted by Brunei authorities for allegedly installing ATM skimming devices in the sultanate was arrested at Miri Airport on Monday. Miri police chief ACP Mohd Farhan Lee Abdullah said the suspect, a man in his 50s, was detained at about 5.30pm following a joint intelligence-sharing operation between the Royal Malaysia Police (PDRM) and the Royal Brunei Police Force (PPDB), according to Harian Metro . He said the operation was launched after Brunei authorities alerted Malaysian police to the suspect’s alleged involvement in installing skimming devices on several automated teller machines (ATMs) in the country.

items.title: Kemas to widen Tabika teacher recruitment criteria beyond early childhood diploma holders
items.summary: PUTRAJAYA, July 23 — The Community Development Department (KEMAS) is discussing with the Public Service Department (JPA) and Public Service Commission (SPA) plans to widen the eligibility criteria for Tabika (nursery school) teacher applicants. KEMAS director-general Datuk Mohd Hanafiah Man said the move aimed to ensure future recruitment of Tabika teachers would not be limited only to holders of a Diploma in Early Childhood Education. Mohd Hanafiah said KEMAS was also strengthening the professional development of Tabika teachers through the recognition of the Early Childhood Education Certificate (SPAK) as a Micro-Credential qualification, in collaboration with Universiti Poly-Tech Malaysia (UPTM).

items.title: Anwar questions rush among rivals to claim Negeri Sembilan MB post
items.summary: SEREMBAN, July 23 — Pakatan Harapan (PH) chairman Datuk Seri Anwar Ibrahim appeared to question what he described as the eagerness of certain parties to vie for the menteri besar’s post. Also present were Negeri Sembilan Menteri Besar Datuk Seri Aminuddin Harun, who is also the PH Linggi candidate in the upcoming Negeri Sembilan state election, PH Sikamat candidate Nor Azman Mohamad and PH Ampangan candidate Datuk Muhammad Nazri Kassim. In this election, PH is contesting all 36 state seats, with PKR fielding 16 candidates, DAP 11 and Amanah nine.

items.title: Lorry, motorcycle crash snarls traffic on LKSA towards Shah Alam
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR, July 23 — A crash involving a lorry and a motorcycle caused kilometres-long traffic congestion on the Kemuning-Shah Alam Highway (LKSA) this morning. Traffic updates at 7.30am said the accident at Kilometre 5.7 near Bulatan Selangor had led to heavy congestion for motorists travelling from Kemuning Bayu towards Shah Alam city centre, Kosmo! Earlier, at 7.04am, highway users were advised to expect delays of up to 40 minutes from the Sri Muda toll plaza to Shah Alam city centre.
